title: Agoseris coronopifolia
taxonomy: Agoseris coronopifolia is classified within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the sunflower or daisy family. It belongs to the genus Agoseris, a group of annual or perennial flowering plants native to North America. Agoseris coronopifolia is further classified as a species within the genus Agoseris, which contains approximately five to seven species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally recognized as a member of the Asteraceae family. Agoseris coronopifolia is also known as crown-of-rocks or western crown-of-rocks, which is a common name that refers to its distinctive appearance.
uses: Agoseris coronopifolia, also known as Greenstem Needle Milkwort, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes for its purported health benefits. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In addition, Agoseris coronopifolia is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The plant's use is not supported by modern scientific evidence, and its effectiveness is largely based on anecdotal reports. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties of Agoseris coronopifolia.
benefits: Agoseris coronopifolia, also known as crown of gold or golden crown, is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's roots are believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate pain and reduce swelling. properties: Agoseris coronopifolia, also known as crown-of-rocks or goldeneye, has been traditionally used by indigenous communities for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots and flowers have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The roots are said to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which may help in soothing wounds and reducing pain. constituents: Agoseris coronopifolia contains a variety of bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds are known to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Sesquiterpene lactones, in particular, have been shown to exhibit cytotoxic and antimicrobial activities. Flavonoids present in the plant are also known to have antiviral and antiallergic properties. The combination of these bioactive compounds contributes to the plant's medicinal properties.

intro: Agoseris coronopifolia, also known as Greenwood or Rocky Mountain cotton-top, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and its extracts have been found to exhibit potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Agoseris coronopifolia include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Agoseris coronopifolia has been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and wounds.
